layout.h(3) Library Functions Manual layout.h(3)

NAME

layout.h - Two-column layout helpers for help text and option formatting.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH 75
#define LAYOUT_DESCRIPTION_START_COL 79
#define LAYOUT_NARROW_TERMINAL_THRESHOLD 55

Functions


void layout_print_wrapped_description (FILE *stream, const char *text, int indent_width, int term_width, int continuation_indent_extra)
Print text with wrapping and proper indentation. void layout_print_two_column_row (FILE *stream, const char *first_column, const char *second_column, int first_col_len, int term_width, int continuation_indent_extra)
Print two-column row with automatic wrapping.

Detailed Description

Two-column layout helpers for help text and option formatting.

Provides reusable functions for formatting two-column layouts with proper alignment and text wrapping. Used by both mode-level help and binary-level help.

Definition in file layout.h.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define LAYOUT_COLUMN_WIDTH 75

Maximum width for first column before description moves to next line

Definition at line 19 of file layout.h.

Referenced by options_config_calculate_max_col_width().

#define LAYOUT_DESCRIPTION_START_COL 79

Starting column for description text (column 79 = 2 spaces + 75 chars + 2 spacing)

Definition at line 22 of file layout.h.

#define LAYOUT_NARROW_TERMINAL_THRESHOLD 55

Terminal width threshold for switching to vertical layout

Definition at line 25 of file layout.h.

Function Documentation

void layout_print_two_column_row (FILE * stream, const char * first_column, const char * second_column, int first_col_len, int term_width, int continuation_indent_extra)

Print two-column row with automatic wrapping. Prints first column text, then second column description with proper alignment. If first column is too long (>45 chars), description moves to next line. Second column wraps at description start column.

Parameters

stream Output file stream
first_column Text for first column
second_column Description for second column
first_col_len Length of first column text (excluding ANSI codes)
term_width Terminal width
continuation_indent_extra Extra spaces for continuation lines (0 for none, 2 for options)

Definition at line 220 of file layout.c.

221                                                                                 {
222   if (!stream || !first_column || !second_column)
223     return;
224 
225   // Strip ANSI escape codes before calculating display width
226   char *stripped = ansi_strip_escapes(first_column, strlen(first_column));
227 
228   // Calculate actual display width of first column (accounts for UTF-8, excluding ANSI codes)
229   int display_width = utf8_display_width(stripped ? stripped : first_column);
230   if (display_width < 0)
231     display_width = first_col_len; // Fallback to provided value if available
232 
233   SAFE_FREE(stripped);
234 
235   // Use calculated first_col_len if provided (accounts for UTF-8 and ANSI codes),
236   // otherwise default to 35. The provided width respects both colored text and UTF-8 characters.
237   // Allow the first column to expand up to 60% of terminal width, but leave room for description.
238   int fixed_first_col_width = (first_col_len > 0) ? first_col_len : 35;
239   int max_first_col_width = (term_width * 6) / 10;
240   if (max_first_col_width < 40)
241     max_first_col_width = 40;
242   if (fixed_first_col_width > max_first_col_width) {
243     fixed_first_col_width = max_first_col_width;
244   }
245 
246   // Second column starts after first column (with 2 spaces padding)
247   // This allows descriptions to be quite wide for wrapping
248   int second_col_start = 2 + fixed_first_col_width + 2;
249 
250   // At narrow terminal widths, force single-column layout
251   bool force_single_column = (term_width <= 90);
252 
253   // Position where first column ends (including leading spaces)
254   int first_col_end = 2 + display_width;
255 
256   // Check if first column fits within the fixed width
257   bool fits_in_first_column_width = (display_width <= fixed_first_col_width);
258 
259   // If first column fits within its width and we're not forcing single column, try same-line layout
260   if (fits_in_first_column_width && !force_single_column) {
261     // Print first column
262     fprintf(stream, "  %s", first_column);
263 
264     // Pad to second column start position
265     int padding = second_col_start - first_col_end;
266     if (padding > 0) {
267       fprintf(stream, "%*s", padding, "");
268     } else {
269       fprintf(stream, " ");
270     }
271 
272     // Print description with wrapping at second column position
273     layout_print_wrapped_description(stream, second_column, second_col_start, term_width, continuation_indent_extra);
274     fprintf(stream, "\n");
275   } else if (force_single_column) {
276     // Terminal too narrow, put description on next line with indent
277     fprintf(stream, "  %s\n", first_column);
278 
279     // Only print description if it exists
280     if (second_column && second_column[0] != '\0') {
281       int description_indent = 4;
282       for (int i = 0; i < description_indent; i++)
283         fprintf(stream, " ");
284 
285       layout_print_wrapped_description(stream, second_column, description_indent, term_width,
286                                        continuation_indent_extra);
287       fprintf(stream, "\n");
288     }
289   } else {
290     // First column overflows its fixed width - bump description to next line
291     // Print first column as-is (it will overflow)
292     fprintf(stream, "  %s\n", first_column);
293 
294     // Only print description if it exists
295     if (second_column && second_column[0] != '\0') {
296       // Print description on next line at second column position, with wide wrapping
297       for (int i = 0; i < second_col_start; i++)
298         fprintf(stream, " ");
299       layout_print_wrapped_description(stream, second_column, second_col_start, term_width, continuation_indent_extra);
300       fprintf(stream, "\n");
301     }
302   }
303 }




References ansi_strip_escapes(), display_width(),
    layout_print_wrapped_description(), SAFE_FREE, and
    utf8_display_width().


Referenced by
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(),
    options_config_print_usage(), and
    options_print_help_for_mode().






void
  layout_print_wrapped_description (FILE * stream, const char * text, int
  indent_width, int term_width, int continuation_indent_extra)


Print text with wrapping and proper indentation. Wraps text to fit
    within terminal width, maintaining specified indentation for wrapped lines.
    Applies color formatting to metadata labels.




Parameters


stream Output file stream


text Plain text to print (may contain wrapping markers)


indent_width Number of spaces to indent wrapped lines


term_width Terminal width (0 for default 80)


continuation_indent_extra Extra spaces to add to continuation lines (0
  for none, 2 for options)


Definition at line 98 of file layout.c.


99                                                                      {
100   if (!text || !stream)
101     return;
102 
103   // Default terminal width if not specified
104   if (term_width <= 0)
105     term_width = 80;
106 
107   // Available width for text after indentation, capped at 130 for readability
108   // Account for continuation lines having extra indent, so reduce available width accordingly
109   // This allows the description column itself to be up to 130 chars wide,
110   // starting from indent_width, for a max total line of indent_width + 130 (first line)
111   // or indent_width + continuation_indent_extra + 130 (continuation lines)
112   int available_width = term_width - indent_width - continuation_indent_extra;
113   if (available_width > 130)
114     available_width = 130;
115   if (available_width < 20)
116     available_width = 20;
117 
118   const char *line_start = text;
119   const char *last_space = NULL;
120   const char *p = text;
121   bool inside_metadata = false;
122 
123   while (*p) {
124     // Check for explicit newline
125     if (*p == '\n') {
126       // Print everything up to the newline
127       int text_len = p - line_start;
128       if (text_len > 0) {
129         char seg[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
130         SAFE_STRNCPY(seg, line_start, text_len + 1);
131         layout_print_colored_segment(stream, seg);
132       }
133 
134       fprintf(stream, "\n");
135       if (*(p + 1)) {
136         // Continuation lines get extra indent if specified
137         int continuation_indent = indent_width + continuation_indent_extra;
138         for (int i = 0; i < continuation_indent; i++)
139           fprintf(stream, " ");
140       }
141       p++;
142       line_start = p;
143       last_space = NULL;
144       inside_metadata = false;
145       continue;
146     }
147 
148     // Check for start of metadata blocks (must check before space tracking)
149     // Use helper that accounts for ANSI codes in the metadata
150     if (!inside_metadata && is_metadata_start(p)) {
151       inside_metadata = true;
152     }
153 
154     // Check for end of metadata blocks - always exit on )
155     // We'll re-enter when we see the next metadata marker
156     if (inside_metadata && *p == ')') {
157       inside_metadata = false;
158     }
159 
160     // Track spaces for word wrapping, including inside metadata blocks
161     // Prefer breaks before metadata blocks by updating last_space even if we already have one
162     if (*p == ' ') {
163       const char *next = p + 1;
164       while (*next == ' ')
165         next++;
166       // Track space as break point if it's before metadata or a normal space (not before parenthetical)
167       if (is_metadata_start(next) || !last_space || *next != '(') {
168         last_space = p;
169       }
170     }
171 
172     // Decode UTF-8 character length
173     int char_bytes = utf8_next_char_bytes(p, strlen(p));
174 
175     if (char_bytes <= 0) {
176       // Invalid UTF-8 or end of string, stop
177       break;
178     }
179 
180     // Advance to next character first
181     p += char_bytes;
182 
183     // Calculate actual display width from line_start to current position (excluding ANSI codes)
184     int actual_width = calculate_segment_display_width(line_start, p - line_start);
185 
186     // Check if we need to wrap
187     if (actual_width >= available_width && last_space && last_space > line_start) {
188       // Print text up to break point with colors applied
189       int text_len = last_space - line_start;
190       char seg[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
191       SAFE_STRNCPY(seg, line_start, text_len + 1);
192       layout_print_colored_segment(stream, seg);
193 
194       fprintf(stream, "\n");
195       // Continuation lines get extra indent if specified
196       int continuation_indent = indent_width + continuation_indent_extra;
197       for (int i = 0; i < continuation_indent; i++)
198         fprintf(stream, " ");
199 
200       p = last_space + 1;
201       line_start = p;
202       last_space = NULL;
203       continue;
204     }
205   }
206 
207   // Print remaining text
208   if (p > line_start) {
209     char seg[BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM];
210     int text_len = p - line_start;
211     SAFE_STRNCPY(seg, line_start, text_len + 1);
212     layout_print_colored_segment(stream, seg);
213   }
214 }






References BUFFER_SIZE_MEDIUM, SAFE_STRNCPY, and
    utf8_next_char_bytes().


Referenced by layout_print_two_column_row(), and
    options_print_help_for_mode().
